name: P4-reconciliation-question
description: How-to question about a one-cent reconciliation discrepancy. Customer already knows the cause, just wants the recommended fix. Should produce high confidence + a single short next step.
parserText:
COID/MID: 7903 / 0061-8842
CASE: ESC-2026-0510-P4E
CLIENT/CONTACT: Iverson Bookkeeping Services - Sam I.
CX IS ATTEMPTING TO: Finish April reconciliation for Northshore Auto Detail. There is a $0.01 ending-balance variance against the bank statement.
EXPECTED OUTCOME: Reconciliation completes at $0.00 difference and shows a green 'Success' banner so Sam can move on to the next client.
ACTUAL OUTCOME: The reconcile screen shows '$0.01 Difference - Cannot finish without resolving'. Sam already knows it came from a rounded sales-tax line and just wants the recommended way to clear it cleanly without distorting the P&L.
KB/TOOLS USED: Reconciliation help article, bank statement PDF, the customer's own internal bookkeeping notes.
TRIED TEST ACCOUNT: Not applicable - this is a routine procedural question.
TS STEPS: Verified the variance is exactly $0.01. Confirmed it traces back to a single sales-tax adjustment posted on 04/22/2026. Asked Sam to confirm no other transactions are unreconciled. Sam wants the cleanest path - either a one-cent expense account entry or a Reconciliation Discrepancies adjustment - whichever your team typically recommends for under-a-dollar variances.
